Class PrimitiveConversions

java.lang.Object
dev.dejvokep.boostedyaml.utils.conversion.PrimitiveConversions
public class PrimitiveConversions extends Object
Utility class for primitives.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>>
    NON_NUMERIC_CONVERSIONS
    All non-numeric primitives and their corresponding non-primitive representations; vice-versa.
    static final Set<Class<?>>
    NUMERIC_CLASSES
    All numeric data type classes.
    static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>>
    NUMERIC_PRIMITIVES
    All numeric primitives and their corresponding non-primitive representations.
    static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>>
    PRIMITIVES_TO_OBJECTS
    All primitives mapped to their object representations.

  • Field Details

    • NUMERIC_PRIMITIVES

      public static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> NUMERIC_PRIMITIVES
      All numeric primitives and their corresponding non-primitive representations.

    • PRIMITIVES_TO_OBJECTS

      public static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> PRIMITIVES_TO_OBJECTS
      All primitives mapped to their object representations.

    • NON_NUMERIC_CONVERSIONS

      public static final Map<Class<?>,Class<?>> NON_NUMERIC_CONVERSIONS
      All non-numeric primitives and their corresponding non-primitive representations; vice-versa.

    • NUMERIC_CLASSES

      public static final Set<Class<?>> NUMERIC_CLASSES
      All numeric data type classes.

  • Constructor Details

    • PrimitiveConversions

      public PrimitiveConversions()

  • Method Details

    • isNumber

      public static boolean isNumber(@NotNull Class<?> clazz)
      Returns if the given class represents a numeric data type.
      Parameters:
      clazz - the class to check
      Returns:
      if it's a numeric class

    • convertNumber

      public static Object convertNumber(@NotNull Object value, @NotNull Class<?> target)
      Converts a number to the target type. It must be guaranteed that the given object is an instance of Number and NUMERIC_CLASSES must contain the target class.

      This method supports casting between two numeric primitives, two non-primitive numeric representations and one of each kind. Casting between any primitive type, and it's non-primitive representation is also supported.

      Parameters:
      value - the value to convert
      target - the target type
      Returns:
      the converted number

    • toInt

      public static Optional<Integer> toInt(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to integer.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via Integer.valueOf(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the integer

    • toByte

      public static Optional<Byte> toByte(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to byte.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via Byte.valueOf(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the byte

    • toLong

      public static Optional<Long> toLong(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to long.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via Long.valueOf(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the long

    • toDouble

      public static Optional<Double> toDouble(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to double.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via Double.valueOf(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the double

    • toFloat

      public static Optional<Float> toFloat(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to float.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via Float.valueOf(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the float

    • toShort

      public static Optional<Short> toShort(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to short.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via Short.valueOf(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the short

    • toBigInt

      public static Optional<BigInteger> toBigInt(@Nullable Object value)
      Converts the given value to big integer.

      If the given value is null, not an instance of BigInteger, Number or could not be converted by parsing from string via BigInteger(String), returns an empty optional.

      Parameters:
      value - the number
      Returns:
      the big integer
